perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information of Disclosing Party pursuant to a valid order issued by a court or governmental agency; prov ided that Receiving Party only discloses that portion of the Confidential Information that is specifical ly required by the order, and further provided that Receiving Party provides Disclosing Party with: (a) prior written notice of such order, and (b) reasonable opportunity to oppose such disclosure or obtain a pr otective order or similar relief. Receiving Party agrees to cooperate with Di sclosing Party in seeking any such protective order or similar relief.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party may disclose the Confidential Information of Disclosing Party pursuant to a valid order issued by a court or governmental agency; Receiving Party is NOT REQUIRED to provides Disclosing Party with: (a) prior written notice of such order, and (b) reasonable opportunity to oppose such disclosure or obtain a pr otective order or similar relief. Receiving Party DOES NOT need to cooperate with Di sclosing Party in seeking any such protective order or similar relief.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the obligation for the Receiving Party to notify the Disclosing Party of a court order and cooperate in seeking a protective order, it directly conflicts with Section 10, which states that Receiving Party may issue a statement that contains Confidential Information if required to do so by applicable laws and if it advises Disclosing Party, at least forty-eight hours in advance, of such pending disclosure and consults with Disclosing Party as to the contents of the statement. Removing requirements contradicts with each other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party hereto agrees, and shall cause its Representativ es, not to announce or disclose to any third person its partici pation in discussions with the o ther party concerning the matters contained in this Agreement without the advance written consent of the other Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may issue a statement that contains Confidentia l Information if, in the opinion of its legal counsel, it is required to do so by applicable laws and if it a dvises Disclosing Party, at least forty-eight hours in advance, of such pending disclosure and consults with Disclosing Party as to the contents of the statement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Receiving Party is NOT REQUIRED to provide notice or cooperate in seeking a protective order. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Receiving Party may issue a statement containing Confidential Information if it advises Disclosing Party at least forty-eight hours in advance of such pending disclosure and consults with Disclosing Party as to the contents of the statement. The change removes obligations that are required in the contradicted text, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information sh all remain the exclusive prop erty of Disclosing Party unless such Confidential Information is sold by Disclosing Part y to Receiving Party. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring to Receiving Party or any of its Representatives any rights by license or otherwise in any Confi dential Information, or under any trademark, patent, patent application, design work, copyright, trade secret or any other property right now owned or hereafter owned or cont rolled by Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information shall become the shared property of both the Disclosing Party and Receiving Party. This Agreement shall be construed as granting and conferring to Receiving Party rights by license or otherwise in any Confidential Information, or under any trademark, patent, patent application, design work, copyright, trade secret or any other property right now owned or hereafter owned or controlled by Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that Confidential Information becomes the shared property of both parties and explicitly granting rights to the Receiving Party, this contradicts Section 2, which outlines the Receiving Party's obligations to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information and use it only for a specific purpose. It goes against the obligations previously mentioned for Confidentiality and non-disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon receipt of any Confidential Information, Receiving Part y shall: (a) only disclose such Confidential Information to those of its Representatives with a need to have access to and knowledge of the Confidential Inform ation for the limited purpose expressly stated herein and not disclose to any other person ex cept with the prior written approval of Disclosing Party; (b) advise any of its Representatives who receive the Confident ial Information of the existence and terms of this Agreement and of the obligations of confidentiality and non- disclosure herein; cause its Representatives to comply with thi s Agreement as if they were parties hereto; and be responsible for a ny breach of this Agreement by its Representatives; (c) use and require its Representatives to use at least the sam e degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as they use to protect the ir own proprietary information, which degree of care shall not be less than reasonable care; an d
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that Confidential Information becomes shared property and grants rights to the Receiving Party. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which imposes strict obligations on the Receiving Party to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information and only use it for a limited purpose. The change breaks compatibility with the confidentiality and non-disclosure obligations, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receivi ng Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, immedi ately return (or, at the op tion of Disclosing Party, destroy) all tangible or electronic Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving P arty or any of its Representatives under this Agreement, along with all copies, extracts and reproductio ns of such information and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans , enhancements, data, prototypes, derivative works and all other works prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that are derived from or based on any of the Confidential Information. Receiving Part y further agrees to promptly provide Disclosing Party with written certification of the satisfaction of the terms of this paragraph by Receiving Party and each of its Representatives that had possession or co ntrol of any Confidential Information. Provided, however, that Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information, subject to the restrictions on use and disclosure contained in this Agreement, in accordance Commercial Investigations LLC Page 3 of 4 with their respective internal record retention policies for le gal, compliance or regulatory purposes or to establish the rights of Receiving Party and its Representatives under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receiving Party has the option to return or destroy all tangible or electronic Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party or any of its Representatives under this Agreement, along with all copies, extracts and reproductions of such information and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans, enhancements, data, prototypes, derivative works and all other works prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that are derived from or based on any of the Confidential Information. Receiving Party is not required to provide Disclosing Party with written certification of the satisfaction of the terms of this paragraph by Receiving Party and each of its Representatives that had possession or control of any Confidential Information. Receiving Party and its Representatives are required to destroy copies of Confidential Information and are NOT allow to retain any copies of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the Receiving Party has the option to return the information, this change directly contradicts the statement in Section 14, which says, All obligations of Receiving Party with respect to any Conf idential Information received from Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement shall s urvive and continue for five (5) years after the date of the last discl osure of Confidential Informati on by Disclosing Party. If the Receiving party doesn't return or destroy the confidentnial information, this contradict Section 14
perturbation.contradicted_text: All obligations of Receiving Party with respect to any Conf idential Information received from Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement shall s urvive and continue for five (5) years after the date of the last discl osure of Confidential Informati on by Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the original agreement regarding the handling of confidential information upon demand. The original text stipulates the Receiving Party 'shall' return or destroy the information, while the changed text states they 'have the option' to do so. Furthermore, the original text requires written certification of compliance, which is removed in the changed text. Most importantly, the original text allows for retention of copies under specific conditions, while the changed text explicitly prohibits retention. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that obligations regarding confidential information survive for five years, because if the Receiving party has the option to destroy or not return the information, that contradicts Section 14.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party agrees that it shall not, and shall cause it Representative not to, remove, overprint or deface any notice of confidentiality, copyright, t rademark, logo, legend or other notices of ownership or confidentiality from any original or copies of the Confidential Information it obtains from Disclosing Party, or authorize any third party to do any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party is permitted to remove, overprint or deface any notice of confidentiality, copyright, trademark, logo, legend or other notices of ownership or confidentiality from any original or copies of the Confidential Information it obtains from Disclosing Party, and can authorize any third party to do any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: By allowing the Receiving Party to alter or remove confidentiality notices, it conflicts directly with Section 2, which imposes a duty on the Receiving Party to protect the confidentiality of the Disclosing Party's information and to ensure that its representatives comply with the Agreement's terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon receipt of any Confidential Information, Receiving Part y shall: (a) only disclose such Confidential Information to those of its Representatives with a need to have access to and knowledge of the Confidential Inform ation for the limited purpose expressly stated herein and not disclose to any other person ex cept with the prior written approval of Disclosing Party; (b) advise any of its Representatives who receive the Confident ial Information of the existence and terms of this Agreement and of the obligations of confidentiality and non- disclosure herein; cause its Representatives to comply with thi s Agreement as if they were parties hereto; and be responsible for a ny breach of this Agreement by its Representatives; (c) use and require its Representatives to use at least the sam e degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as they use to protect the ir own proprietary information, which degree of care shall not be less than reasonable care; an d
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows the Receiving Party to remove confidentiality notices, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that requires the Receiving Party to ensure their Representatives are aware of and comply with confidentiality obligations. The changed text undermines the protection of Confidential Information as required by the contradicted text, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is not assignable and is binding on any successors of Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is freely assignable by the Receiving Party and is NOT binding on any successors of Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By stating the agreement is freely assignable, this change contradicts Section 11, which talks about Receiving Party acknowledges that any disclosure or misappr opriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cau se Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, a grees that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief in addition to other remedies and relief t hat would be available at law or in equity without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting a bo nd. If the agreement can be assigned, then it is unclear who is liable for disclosure of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that any disclosure or misappr opriation of any of the Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cau se Disclosing Party irreparable harm, the amount of which may be difficult to ascertain and, therefore, a grees that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief in addition to other remedies and relief t hat would be available at law or in equity without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting a bo nd. In the event of any action for breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party or its Representatives, th e prevailing party shall be entitled to reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses incurred in such action.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement is 'not assignable'. The changed text makes the agreement 'freely assignable'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which holds the 'Receiving Party' responsible for breaches. If the agreement is freely assignable, it creates uncertainty about who the 'Receiving Party' is and who is liable for breaches, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.
